extraction.one_line_summary: CICONE Giulio soloed from breakaway over Col du Port with 3km to go, held off VINGEGAARD by 23 seconds on technical descent to finish
tactical_patterns: CICONE attacked breakaway partner Alaphilippe final 3km Col du Port, prioritizing descent advantage over summit cooperation.
CASTRO VIEJO bridged 90-second gap from peloton to breakaway on beyond-category climb, showing Ineos commitment to stage hunting.
VINGEGAARD personally led out LAPORTE in stage 1 sprint despite GC leadership, revealing team confidence in already-secured race control.

rider_signals.CICONE Giulio: positive: exploited Alaphilippe's early breakaway work then dropped him with 3km climbing, held descending gap against charging GC riders
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: had legs for 120km breakaway aggression from 7th overall but cracked when Cicone attacked final 3km climb
rider_signals.CASTRO VIEJO Jonathan: positive: bridged 90-second gap solo from peloton to breakaway on beyond-category climb, though later dropped before finale
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: commentary noted performance 'subpar' by pre-crash standards but strong for comeback trajectory, improving through race
rider_signals.PEDRERO Antonio: negative: attacked penultimate climb aggressively but lacked legs to follow Cicone, couldn't sustain effort to finale
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: climbed to podium position while Yates, Hindley finished close, suggesting genuine GC form not just diesel consistency
